title: GDPR – Privacy Notice
description: Privacy notice on the processing of personal data by the Court Bailiff at the District Court in Otwock, Marcin Starzec.
content:
PRIVACY NOTICE ON THE PROCESSING OF PERSONAL DATA BY THE COURT BAILIFF AT THE DISTRICT COURT IN OTWOCK, MARCIN STARZEC

(pursuant to Art. 13 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 27 April 2016 on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data, and repealing Directive 95/46/EC (hereinafter: GDPR) (OJ EU L 119 of 04.05.2016) and the Act of 10 May 2018 on the Protection of Personal Data (Journal of Laws of 2018, item 1000)).

Controller

The controller is the Court Bailiff at the District Court in Otwock, Marcin Starzec. Contact details: ul. Stanisława Staszica 27 lok. 41, 05-400 Otwock; e-mail: otwock.starzec@komornik.pl

Data Protection Officer

Contact details: Data Protection Officer, ul. Stanisława Staszica 27/41, 05-400 Otwock; e-mail: iod.otwock.starzec@komornik.pl

Purposes and Legal Basis for Processing Personal Data

For the purposes of the bailiff's activities as a body conducting enforcement and security proceedings and performing other statutory tasks, the controller processes personal data for the purpose of: fulfilling a legal obligation imposed on the controller (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR), performing a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller (Art. 6(1)(e) GDPR). The legal basis consists of: the Act of 17 November 1964 — Code of Civil Procedure (Journal of Laws 1964, No. 43, item 296, as amended), the Act of 22 March 2018 on Court Bailiffs (Journal of Laws 2018, item 771, as amended), the Regulation of the Minister of Justice of 18 December 2018 on detailed rules for maintaining records, accounting, and financial operations records of bailiff offices (Journal of Laws 2018, item 2517), and the Regulation of the Minister of Justice of 14 December 2018 on the storage and destruction of bailiff case files and closed registry devices (Journal of Laws 2018, item 2408).

For office operation purposes, the controller processes personal data on a legal basis for the purposes of: performing a contract to which the data subject is a party, or taking steps at the request of the data subject prior to entering into a contract (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR); fulfilling a legal obligation imposed on the controller (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR); pursuing legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR: e.g. ensuring IT and network security, fraud prevention, claims enforcement; Art. 6(1)(f) does not apply to processing carried out by public authorities in the performance of their tasks). The legal basis for processing consists of labour law, social insurance, accounting, tax, and civil law regulations. Where personal data processing is not necessary for the above purposes, processing is based on the consent of the data subject (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR).

Recipients or Categories of Recipients

Recipients include: parties to proceedings and their attorneys or representatives; debtors of seized receivables (obligated debtor, bank, employer, brokerage entity, drawee, and other entities executing, at the enforcement authority's request, the seizure of receivables or other property rights of the obligated party); entities listed in Art. 761 CCP (including public administration bodies, tax authorities, social insurance authorities, banks, credit unions, brokerage companies, residential community boards, as well as other institutions and persons not participating in the proceedings) from which the bailiff may obtain information necessary for conducting enforcement; recipients of auction notices and auction participants; experts; witnesses to enforcement activities; courts and public prosecutors.

Personal data may be entrusted to processing entities under a data processing agreement or other legal instrument, including for the purpose of providing the controller with IT, accounting, postal, and banking services.

Categories of Personal Data

This information applies to personal data obtained in a manner other than directly from the data subject.

The controller may process ordinary personal data, special categories of personal data, and personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences.

Information on Transfer of Personal Data to Third Countries and Automated Decision-Making

The controller does not intend to transfer personal data to a third country or international organisation, unless a party/participant in the proceedings is an entity whose place of residence, seat, or correspondence address is outside the European Economic Area.

The controller does not apply automated decision-making, including profiling.

Data Retention Period

The retention period for bailiff case files and closed registry devices results from the Regulation of the Minister of Justice of 14 December 2018 on the storage and destruction of bailiff case files and closed registry devices (Journal of Laws 2018, item 2408) and is 10 or 12 years for category A documentation and 5 years for category B documentation, calculated from the beginning of the calendar year following the year in which the proceedings were concluded or the registry device was closed.

The retention period for data processed for tax, accounting, labour law, or social insurance purposes results from, inter alia, the Tax Ordinance (and/or the Accounting Act), the Act on Retirement and Disability Pensions from the Social Insurance Fund, and the Social Insurance System Act.

Source of Personal Data

This information applies to personal data obtained in a manner other than directly from the data subject.

Personal data are obtained from parties to proceedings and their attorneys or representatives; witnesses to activities; debtors of seized receivables (e.g. employers, banks); entities listed in Art. 761 CCP (the enforcement authority may request participants in the proceedings to provide explanations; the enforcement authority may request information on the debtor's financial condition or enabling identification of their assets, as well as address data necessary for the proper conduct of the proceedings, from public administration bodies, entities performing public administration tasks, tax authorities, social insurance authorities, banks, credit unions, insurance or reinsurance companies, entities managing securities accounts, housing cooperatives, residential communities and other entities managing premises, business information bureaus, as well as other institutions and persons not participating in the proceedings); from publicly available information sources and registers accessible electronically (Art. 801 CCP).

Rights of Data Subjects

The data subject has the right to request from the controller access to their personal data, rectification, erasure ('right to be forgotten') or restriction of processing, or the right to object, subject to the following:

  • the right of access to data is exercised in accordance with Art. 9 CCP and subject to the bailiff's obligation of professional secrecy;
  • the right to rectification of data is exercised in accordance with the CCP;
  • the right to restriction of processing is exercised exclusively in accordance with Art. 767 CCP (i.e. via complaint against the bailiff's actions);
  • the right to erasure does not apply where retention is necessary, inter alia, for the controller to fulfil a legal obligation or perform a task carried out in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority vested in the controller, or to establish, pursue, or defend legal claims (Art. 17(3) GDPR);
  • where justified by a special situation of the data subject, they may object to processing for the purpose of performing a task in the public interest (under Art. 6(1)(e) GDPR) or for the controller's legitimate interests (under Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR);
  • where processing is based on consent, the data subject has the right to withdraw consent at any time without affecting the lawfulness of processing carried out prior to withdrawal;
  • where processing is carried out automatically on the basis of consent or a contract, the data subject has the right to data portability.

Right to Lodge a Complaint with a Supervisory Authority

The supervisory authority with which complaints may be lodged regarding the processing of personal data by the court bailiff is the President of the Personal Data Protection Office (UODO).

Information on the Requirement to Provide Data and Consequences of Not Providing It

Providing personal data for the purposes of the bailiff's tasks as an enforcement authority is a statutory requirement, specified in Art. 761 and 801 CCP. A person responsible for unjustifiably refusing to provide the enforcement authority with explanations or information provided in Art. 761, or for knowingly providing false information or explanations, may be fined by the enforcement authority at the creditor's request or on its own motion.
